name: merge
description: Automates the integration of a completed Step 5 feature branch or specific stable commit into 'main', and pushes the result to origin — without spending any LLM reasoning turns. Verifies the target is clean, pushed, in-history, and a Step 5 'Code and Spec Approved' commit before merging. If a dedicated 'main' worktree exists it merges there (untouched feature checkout); otherwise it falls back to an in-place single-checkout merge that always returns you to your feature branch (and rolls back on conflict). Available as the /merge command inside Pi Coding Agent, or as the standalone ./merge / the npm-global `merge` CLI from any other shell, including Claude Code's '!' prefix.

examples.desc: Safely verify, merge, and push the current HEAD commit into main.
Verify, merge, and push the specific stable Step 5 commit 638e9b4 into main.

why.scenario: You just completed a Step 5 feature branch and need to merge it to main without context-switching to another terminal.
why.result: The HEAD commit is validated (clean worktree, pushed, Step 5 approved), merged into main, and pushed to origin.

why.scenario: You want to merge a specific earlier stable checkpoint (not your current HEAD) — for example, the most recent Step 5 commit in a long branch history.
why.result: That specific commit is validated against the same rules and merged into main.

why.scenario: You have two feature branches and want to merge them into each other (not into main).
why.result: The tool only merges into main. Use standard 'git merge' for inter-branch merges. The merge tool's job is safe Step-5-gated promotion to main — not arbitrary branch merging.

usage.desc: Optional commit hash, branch, or reference to merge (default: HEAD).
Explain why you'd run this tool, with user scenarios and anti-use-cases.
Skip branch-cleanup prompt — auto-delete the feature branch (local + remote) and switch to main after a successful merge, assuming worktree is clean.
